mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals that the real economy is contracting, reducing corporate earnings expectations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines month-over-month but the 20-day forward return on the S&P 500 is positive in more than 50% of observed months, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) crosses above 6.0% after having been below 5.5% within the prior three months, it marks a cyclical deterioration in the labor market that reduces household income and spending, depressing equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 6.0% from below 5.5% within 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE crosses above 6.0% and the S&P 500 one-month forward return is positive in more than 40% of such events, or the average return is not significantly different from zero, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of very low housing starts (HOUST below 1000 thousand units) and a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE above 6.0%) signals a severe feedback loop between housing market collapse and labor market weakness, amplifying the negative impact on consumer wealth and spending, thus driving S&P 500 returns downward over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing bust and rising unemployment co-occur in 2008 recession
failure_condition: If HOUST < 1000 and UNRATE > 6.0% jointly occur but the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is positive more than 30% of the time, or the signal's rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 2-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: During a severe recession when the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is already low (below 2.5%), the central bank's conventional policy ammunition is nearly exhausted; further easing is perceived as ineffective, and the economy's deep distress dominates the outlook, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Policy rate near zero in 2008-2009 crisis
regime_hint: severe recession with near-zero rate expectations
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 2.5% but the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is positive in more than 40% of months in that regime, or the average excess return relative to non-regime periods is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real retail and food service sales (RSAFS) indicates fading consumer spending, which directly reduces corporate revenue and profits; this negative demand signal drives S&P 500 returns lower over a very short 5-trading-day horizon as market participants rapidly reprice earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for ~70% of US GDP
failure_condition: If RSAFS declines month-over-month but the 5-day forward S&P 500 return is positive more than 50% of the time, or the signal has a rank IC below -0.03 over a 1-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) for two consecutive months confirms a weakening labor market that reduces aggregate incomes and consumption; this persistent job loss signal triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as recession fears solidify.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Two consecutive payroll declines typically mark recession onset
failure_condition: If two consecutive monthly declines in PAYEMS occur but the S&P 500 20-day forward return is positive in more than 30% of events, or the average return is not significantly negative, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) above 4.0% indicates elevated inflation that erodes real corporate earnings and purchasing power, and forces a restrictive monetary policy response; this stagflationary environment depresses equity valuations over the longer 60-trading-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Inflation above 4% in 2008 coincided with recession
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL yoy > 4% but the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is positive more than 40% of the time, or the signal's information coefficient is below 0.05 in absolute value, reject.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a year-over-year decline in both real GDP (GDPC1, quarterly) and industrial production (INDPRO, monthly) confirms a broad-based contraction in economic output; this supply-demand collapse signals deep recession and strongly predicts negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 (quarterly) yoy decline AND INDPRO (monthly) yoy decline
evidence_refs: fact:GPD and IP both declining in 2008 recession
failure_condition: If both GDPC1 and INDPRO decline yoy simultaneously but the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is positive more than 25% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05), reject.
